hq: 2261 Market Street STE 22526, San Francisco, CA 94114

system_description.overview: Nexus Intelligence provides an AI-automated manufacturing operations platform designed to help industrial companies improve production, process, and maintenance operations. The platform leverages AI to automate repetitive tasks performed by industrial engineers, improve understanding of equipment logic, and includes workflow management, ingestion and processing of control system and factory software data.

products.Hosted Industrial AI Agent Management: End-to-end agentic workflow build and operation; integration capabilities with client technology stacks; continuous monitoring and alerting
products.Hosted Industrial Equipment Coding Model: AI-driven code understanding and editing for PLC and other control systems; data fusion with structured/unstructured data in client existing technology stacks
products.Support Services: Automation implementation services including onboarding, setup, integration development; ongoing automation support and expert guidance

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; no physical servers — all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (multiple availability zones within Render; failover across AZs mentioned)
additional_cloud: AWS (S3 Buckets, SQS Queue, SNS Email — Prod Account us-west-2 and us-east-1)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in Render VPC environment; segmented VPCs and ACLs. Staging/dev environments are replicas of production, configured to not allow cross-env traffic. Customer accounts are appropriately segmented from each other (CC6.1.3).
network_architecture.api_gateway: Backend API Server (depicted in network diagram on Render account)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via Render to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS (Transport Layer Security) using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Internet traffic flows through internet gateway to Render.com account (us-west-2 production, us-east-1 prod fallover VPC). Render environment hosts Backend API Server, Frontend Server, other backend services. AWS Prod Account (us-west-2, us-east-1) holds S3 Buckets, SQS Queue, SNS Email. Staging/dev are replicas of production with no cross-account connections.

application_architecture.pattern: Hybrid (Render-hosted backend/frontend services + AWS managed services)
application_architecture.environments: Production (us-west-2), Prod Fallover (us-east-1), Staging, Development — staging/dev are replicas of production with ACLs configured to not allow cross-env traffic

databases: Not explicitly named; Active Directory mentioned for identity; database accounts referenced in access controls
file_storage: AWS S3 (Prod Account us-west-2 and us-east-1)
data_storage.backup_frequency: Backups taken and maintained per Backup Policy; frequency not explicitly stated

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Active Directory (referenced in CC6.1.2 for server/database accounts); internal unique login IDs
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1). MFA also required for source code tool access.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Passwords controlled through Render password policy; periodic forced changes, expiry and complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited failed logon attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest (CC6.1.5)
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider KMS (Render/AWS); access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (name not specified); MFA-protected; branch rules configured
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move change to production requires additional approval (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback; baseline configurations retained as code (CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automated patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations receive automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance; critical/security patches applied as available

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named; monitoring tool generates alerts when predefined thresholds are met
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions and roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure / data center services (VPC, virtual servers, networking)
S3 file storage, SQS queue, SNS email notifications

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous scanning, weekly definition updates) and Mac workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; management reviews high/critical findings
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to facilities: Render is responsible for physical security of data center; excluded from scope
A1.2 — Environmental protections: No office; environmental controls managed by building management / Render
notable_controls: CC5.2.1 — IT team continuously monitors system capacity and performance using monitoring tool with threshold-based alerting
CC6.2.2 — Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged users
CC8.1.3 — Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author; MFA-protected SCM access
CC4.1.1 — Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC7.1.2 — Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications
CC7.4.1 — Annual BCDR testing across multiple loss scenarios

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-region (us-west-2 + us-east-1 failover) with multi-AZ on Render, plus AWS managed services for storage/messaging. Good separation of prod/staging/dev. However, heavy dependence on Render as single primary cloud with limited IaC details disclosed. No CDN mentioned.

application_architecture.rationale: No languages, frameworks, or API style disclosed. Compute architecture visible from network diagram (backend API server, frontend server, other backend services). Branch protection and separate environments confirmed. Pattern appears modular but specifics are sparse.

data_layer.rationale: S3 for file storage confirmed. Specific database technologies not named. No caching or data warehouse mentioned. Backup policy exists but frequency not stated. Data classification and retention policies in place. Per-tenant segregation confirmed.

security_posture.rationale: Strong security controls: IDS/IPS via Render, firewalls, annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, MFA on production and SCM, antivirus on servers and endpoints, quarterly access reviews, 1-day revocation SLA, log protection, annual BCDR testing. Missing: WAF, SIEM, named security tooling.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, separate environments (prod/staging/dev), IaC/configuration management tool confirmed, emergency change process in place. However, CI/CD tool and source control platform not named. No container orchestration mentioned.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, reviewed and tested annually across multiple loss scenarios. Multi-AZ and multi-region failover capability on Render. DR plan includes spinning up multiple servers across AZs. RTO/RPO not disclosed.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only two vendors named: Render (critical, primary compute) and AWS (S3/SQS/SNS). Heavy single-vendor concentration on Render for all primary infrastructure. AWS usage is secondary/supplementary.

overall.rationale: Early-stage industrial AI company with solid governance and security process foundation. Strong BCDR and access control practices. However, heavy Render dependency, sparse technology stack disclosure (no named DB, CI/CD, languages), and Type 1 report limit confidence in operational maturity.

red_flags: Single critical infrastructure dependency on Render — all primary compute, networking, VPC, and IDS/IPS capabilities rely entirely on Render; no named failover to AWS compute
Database technology not disclosed anywhere in the report
CI/CD tool and source control platform not named — only described generically
No WAF mentioned despite web-based platform
Type 1 report only — design suitability assessed, not operating effectiveness over time
yellow_flags: No CDN disclosed for a web-based industrial AI platform
Backup frequency not explicitly stated — only 'per Backup Policy'
RTO and RPO not disclosed despite having BCDR plan
Programming languages and frameworks not disclosed
Only 2 employees shown in org chart (CEO + Lead Architect) — very small team for SOC 2 scope
green_flags: Multi-region architecture with production in us-west-2 and dedicated prod fallover in us-east-1
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with high/critical remediation tracking
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
1 business day access revocation SLA upon termination
MFA enforced for administrative access and source code management
BCDR plan annually tested across multiple loss scenarios
Per-tenant segregation of customer accounts confirmed
Content filtering via proxy server on all systems
key_observations: Nexus serves industrial/manufacturing clients (PLC coding, control systems) — an unusual niche that implies potentially sensitive OT/IT data integration; no specific data classification for OT data mentioned
AWS is used for secondary services (S3, SQS, SNS) while Render handles all primary compute — this hybrid approach is unusual and creates asymmetric dependency risk
The network diagram reveals a 'Frontend Server' on Render alongside a 'Backend API Server' suggesting a traditional two-tier web application, not microservices
Render has its own SOC 2 report which Nexus reviews annually — subservice controls are carved out but Render's security posture is implicitly relied upon for IDS/IPS, VPC, physical security
With only 2 named employees (CEO + Lead Architect), this is extremely early-stage — SOC 2 Type 1 is likely a customer requirement rather than mature compliance program
